title: CORS Handling Quiz
description: Test your knowledge about the critical role of CORS in modern full-stack web development.

questions.question: What does CORS stand for?
options: Cross-Origin Research System
Cross-Origin Resource Sharing
Case-Oriented Reporting Service
Component-Object-Request-Standard

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of CORS?
options: To make the website faster.
To prevent cross-site request forgery attacks.
To identify the same origin for a web page and the requested API.
To allow restricted resources on a web page to be requested from another domain.

questions.question: What happens when a cross-origin request is made by a browser?
options: The request is immediately sent without any checks.
The server refuses the request by default.
The browser sends a 'preflight' request using the OPTIONS method.
The browser automatically handles it without the server knowing.

questions.question: Which of these is the most secure way to handle CORS in a production environment?
options: Using the wildcard (*) for all origins.
Specifying an explicit domain in the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header.
Disabling CORS completely.
Only using HTTP instead of HTTPS.

questions.question: Can you use a wildcard (*) if you need to send credentials (like cookies) with a cross-origin request?
options: Yes, it doesn't matter.
No, you must specify an explicit allowed origin when credentials are included.
Only if you're using localhost.
Yes, as long as you're using CORS middleware.
